steps.title: Convert the radical to exponential form
steps.content: The expression $\sqrt[a]{r^{42}}$ can be rewritten as $r^{42/a}$ using the exponent rule: $\sqrt[n]{x^m} = x^{m/n}$. This converts the radical notation to an exponential form we can work with.

steps.title: Set the exponents equal
steps.content: Since $r^{42/a}$ must equal $r^{6/7}$ and $r > 1$, the exponents must be equal: $$\frac{42}{a} = \frac{6}{7}$$

steps.title: Solve for a
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $a$ and by \$7$: $$42 \cdot 7 = 6a$$ $$294 = 6a$$ $$a = \frac{294}{6} = 49$$
